items.myth: Knowledge cutoff means Claude cannot answer anything about recent events.
items.reality: Cutoff bounds pretrained memory—you can still answer about recent facts if you paste sources, use retrieval, or enable tools that fetch current data.
items.example: Asked about yesterday's earnings without data → unreliable; paste the transcript → accurate summary of provided text.

items.myth: Chatting daily moves the knowledge cutoff forward automatically.
items.reality: Cutoff is tied to the model snapshot's training data, not your conversation history—only switching to a newer model release or supplying context changes factual freshness.
items.example: Using the same model ID for a year does not add 2025 world events to weights; upgrading model IDs may update cutoff per Anthropic docs.
